title: User Login and Logout Flow
interaction_flow: === LOGIN FLOW (doGet / doPost delegates to doGet) ===
LoginSrv: extracts params — userName, password, userType = request.getParameter(...)
[invalid admin] LoginSrv → User/Browser: include → login.jsp?message=Login Denied!
UserServiceImpl → DB: SELECT * FROM user WHERE email=? AND password=? (PreparedStatement.executeQuery)
UserServiceImpl → LoginSrv: returns 'valid' OR error message
UserServiceImpl → DB: SELECT * FROM user WHERE email=? AND password=?
LogoutSrv → User/Browser: forward → login.jsp?message=Successfully Logged Out!
key_relationships: LoginSrv instantiates UserServiceImpl (direct new, no DI)
UserServiceImpl depends_on DBUtil (static methods: provideConnection, closeConnection)
LoginSrv reads/writes HttpSession (username, password, usertype, userdata)
LogoutSrv nullifies all HttpSession attributes (username, password, usertype, userdata)

constructors: UserBean(String userName, Long mobileNo, String emailId, String address, int pinCode, String password)

conditions_and_branches: LOGIN: if userType == 'admin' → hardcoded credential check (no DB); else → DB credential check via UserServiceImpl
ADMIN valid: session set + forward adminViewProduct.jsp; invalid: include login.jsp with error
CUSTOMER valid (status='valid'): fetch UserBean, set session, forward userHome.jsp; invalid: forward login.jsp with error
doPost delegates entirely to doGet in both LoginSrv and LogoutSrv

db_queries.isValidCredential: SELECT * FROM user WHERE email=? AND password=?
db_queries.getUserDetails: SELECT * FROM user WHERE email=? AND password=?

important_notes: No token/JWT — pure HttpSession. Admin login is hardcoded (no DB). doPost delegates to doGet. Logout nullifies session attributes but does NOT call session.invalidate(). UserServiceImpl is instantiated directly (no IoC). Password stored/compared in plaintext.
